prompt: /orchestrate What does this repo do? Actually, inspect the code and update the README if it is stale.

expected.route: Start as clarification/direct answer only if pure Q&A, then leave Tier 0 once repo inspection or docs editing is needed.
expected.delegation: Delegate repo inspection to repo_scout or fallback explorer; delegate docs edits to docs_writer or fallback worker.
expected.escalation: Escalate only if the scout cannot find the relevant surface or docs require design/code judgment.
expected.model_routing: Use gpt-5.3-codex-spark for repo_scout discovery and gpt-5.4-mini for docs_writer edits unless code reasoning requires gpt-5.4.
expected.final_review: Root verifies README claims against cited repo evidence before final response.
expected.runtime_fallback: explorer for read-only inspection; worker for docs edits

expected_ledger.escalation: none unless discovery or docs claims require deeper code judgment
expected_ledger.final_review: root checks README claims against repo evidence

prompt: /orchestrate Fix this one-file bug and run the narrowest relevant check.

expected.route: Tier 1 or Tier 2 depending on whether discovery is needed.
expected.delegation: Use mechanic or implementer_simple; fallback to worker with the intended role in the prompt.
expected.escalation: Escalate to implementer only if the patch requires broader judgment or the first worker is stuck.
expected.model_routing: Start mechanic or implementer_simple on gpt-5.3-codex-spark; escalate to gpt-5.4 implementer when ordinary coding judgment is needed.
expected.final_review: Root inspects changed diff and validation summary.

expected_ledger.escalation: gpt-5.4 implementer only when Spark lacks capability or scope grows
expected_ledger.final_review: root inspects diff and focused validation

prompt: /orchestrate The test command fails after the patch. Determine whether it is introduced, pre-existing, flaky, or environmental.

expected.route: Route failed validation to test_triage before debugging unless the root cause is already obvious.
expected.delegation: Use test_triage or fallback worker acting as test_triage.
expected.escalation: Escalate to debugger only after triage cannot classify the failure with evidence.
expected.model_routing: Use gpt-5.4-mini for known test execution, gpt-5.4 for test_triage, and gpt-5.5 for ambiguous debugger work.
expected.final_review: Root reports exact commands, failure classification, and residual uncertainty.

expected_ledger.escalation: gpt-5.5 debugger only after triage cannot classify the failure
expected_ledger.final_review: root checks command evidence and failure classification

prompt: /orchestrate Change authorization behavior for this API and make sure it is safe.

expected.delegation: Use architect and security_auditor before or alongside implementation; fallback to explorer for read-only design/security review.
expected.escalation: Escalate unresolved auth or data-isolation ambiguity narrowly to a stronger reviewer/auditor path.
expected.model_routing: Start architect, security_auditor, reviewer, and any strong implementation on gpt-5.5 because auth risk has high cost of error.
expected.final_review: Root performs mandatory senior review of security claims, validation, and residual risk.
expected.runtime_fallback: explorer for audit/design, worker for implementation

expected_ledger.escalation: stay on gpt-5.5 and narrow unresolved auth/security questions
expected_ledger.final_review: root verifies security claims, tests, and residual risk before finalizing

prompt: /orchestrate Custom agent TOMLs are not callable here. Still orchestrate a repo investigation and small fix.

expected.route: Keep the same delegation loop while mapping intended roles to available built-in agent types.
expected.delegation: Use explorer for read-only scout/review work, worker for implementation/test work, and default for planning/synthesis if needed.
expected.escalation: Escalate by intended role and model/effort even when the callable agent_type is a fallback.
expected.model_routing: Record intended model and actual fallback model in the routing ledger when a pinned model is unavailable.
expected.final_review: Root notes fallback mapping only if useful and still performs final senior review.

expected_ledger.model: intended model plus actual fallback model
expected_ledger.escalation: same intended role at higher model/effort before role switch
expected_ledger.final_review: root confirms fallback did not change acceptance criteria

prompt: /orchestrate A simple Spark-based implementer got stuck on a focused fix. Escalate without broadening the task.

expected.route: Keep the same narrow objective and same intended implementation role before switching specialties.
expected.delegation: Retry the bounded implementation path with the next adequate model/effort before passing off.
expected.escalation: Escalate from gpt-5.3-codex-spark implementer_simple to gpt-5.4 implementer or gpt-5.5 implementer_strong based on ambiguity and risk.
expected.model_routing: Model class escalation is explicit; raise model first if Spark lacks capability, raise effort first if the model is adequate but needs deeper deliberation.
expected.final_review: Root checks that the escalation stayed narrow and that final validation supports the patch.

expected_ledger.runtime_type: worker fallback if custom roles unavailable
expected_ledger.escalation: same narrow objective, higher model/effort, no scope broadening
expected_ledger.final_review: root checks escalation scope and validation

prompt: /orchestrate Both repo-local and global codex-orchestrate skills are visible. Use the right one to inspect and update a small repo doc.

expected.route: Prefer the repo-local codex-orchestrate skill while working in this repository, but keep global sync behavior documented.
expected.delegation: Delegate repo doc inspection to repo_scout and the small edit to docs_writer or fallback worker.
expected.escalation: Escalate only if duplicate visibility creates contradictory instructions or stale installed behavior.
expected.model_routing: Use gpt-5.3-codex-spark for repo_scout and gpt-5.4-mini for docs_writer.
expected.final_review: Root confirms the repo-local source of truth was used and final docs match source evidence.
expected.runtime_fallback: repo-local skill preferred; explorer/worker if custom agents unavailable

expected_ledger.runtime_type: repo-local skill with custom roles or explorer/worker fallback
expected_ledger.escalation: root resolves source-of-truth conflict before continuing if instructions differ
expected_ledger.final_review: root verifies repo-local source of truth and installed-copy drift status

prompt: /orchestrate gpt-5.5 is unavailable for this auth-sensitive change. Continue safely and record fallback.

expected.route: Keep Tier 4 high-risk orchestration and do not downgrade the risk classification.
expected.delegation: Use architect/security_auditor intent with the nearest safe available model and explicit fallback notation.
expected.escalation: Escalate unresolved risk to root decision instead of pretending the fallback has identical capability.
expected.model_routing: Record intended gpt-5.5 and actual fallback model in the routing ledger and dispatch brief.
expected.final_review: Root reports the fallback and any confidence reduction before finalizing.
expected.runtime_fallback: explorer for read-only security/design review if custom roles unavailable

expected_ledger.model: intended gpt-5.5; actual nearest safe available model
expected_ledger.escalation: root decision if fallback cannot support security confidence
expected_ledger.final_review: root explicitly accounts for model fallback and residual risk

prompt: /orchestrate A worker says the patch is done, but final root review finds missing validation.

expected.route: Do not finalize; route the missing validation or blocking issue back into the controller loop.
expected.delegation: Use test_runner for known validation or test_triage/debugger if validation fails ambiguously.
expected.escalation: Escalate only the missing validation issue, not the whole project.
expected.model_routing: Use gpt-5.4-mini for known test execution, gpt-5.4 for triage, and gpt-5.5 for ambiguous debugging.
expected.final_review: Root final review remains failed until validation evidence is present or the blocker is reported.

expected_ledger.escalation: send only the missing validation issue back through the loop
expected_ledger.final_review: blocked until validation evidence exists or blocker is reported

prompt: /orchestrate This broad request is spawning too many branches. Decide whether to keep delegating or narrow scope.

expected.route: Pause fanout and route a cost/risk checkpoint before spawning more agents.
expected.delegation: Use risk_controller or fallback default/explorer to choose the cheapest safe next step.
expected.escalation: Escalate to root decision if the risk_controller cannot define a stop condition.
expected.model_routing: Use gpt-5.4 with medium effort for risk_controller rather than gpt-5.5 unless high-risk judgment is involved.
expected.final_review: Root confirms fanout is bounded and the next route is justified.

expected_ledger.escalation: root decision if no clear stop condition exists
expected_ledger.final_review: root verifies fanout control and scope boundary

prompt: /orchestrate The implementer timed out without changes while building a browser game. Continue correctly without root taking over implementation.

expected.route: Treat timeout, closed agent, or no useful changes as stuck evidence and rerun routing before implementation continues.
expected.delegation: Repair or split the implementation packet and redelegate the same narrow objective to implementer, implementer_strong, or fallback worker; root may only do deterministic micro-actions.
expected.escalation: Escalate the same implementation objective by model class and/or effort; pass off only on role mismatch.
expected.model_routing: Record the prior implementer model/effort, then use a stronger implementation role/model if capability or timeout was the bottleneck.
expected.final_review: Root verifies implementation remained delegated after timeout and validation/review were not used as substitutes for implementation delegation.

expected_ledger.escalation: timeout or no-change result becomes stuck evidence, followed by packet repair and same-objective redelegation
expected_ledger.final_review: root confirms no root takeover occurred after timeout

prompt: /orchestrate The root implemented most of the feature locally, then only delegated validation and review. Detect and block validation-only laundering.

expected.route: Block completion when substantive implementation was not delegated and only validation/review was delegated after the fact.
expected.delegation: Route the substantive implementation objective back to implementer or implementer_strong unless the remaining work is deterministic micro-action.
expected.escalation: Escalate to risk_controller if the root cannot prove implementation delegation happened before validation.
expected.model_routing: Use gpt-5.4 implementer for ordinary implementation and gpt-5.4-mini test_runner for validation; do not count validation alone as implementation delegation.
expected.final_review: Root final review blocks until substantive work has delegated evidence or a blocker is reported.
expected.runtime_fallback: worker for implementation and validation fallback

expected_ledger.escalation: root records laundering blocker and routes implementation or risk decision instead of finalizing
expected_ledger.final_review: blocked because validation-only delegation does not satisfy delegate-first implementation

prompt: /orchestrate Two same-role attempts failed for different reasons. Stop retrying blindly and route the mismatch correctly.

expected.route: After two failed same-role attempts, route to risk_controller, planner, or the correct specialist instead of retrying blindly.
expected.delegation: Use risk_controller for the loop decision and then the correct specialist role for the next bounded step.
expected.escalation: Escalate from repeated same-role failure to role mismatch analysis, not another blind same-role retry.
expected.model_routing: Use gpt-5.4 risk_controller or planner, then gpt-5.5 specialist only if ambiguity or risk requires it.
expected.final_review: Root confirms the repeated failures were classified and the new route matches the evidence.
expected.runtime_fallback: default for risk/planning fallback; worker or explorer for specialist fallback

expected_ledger.escalation: role mismatch loop triggers risk_controller instead of blind retry
expected_ledger.final_review: root verifies loop recovery and next route evidence

prompt: /orchestrate Two subagents disagree about the source of a failing test. Resolve the conflict with evidence before continuing.

expected.route: Route contradictory outputs to reviewer, architect, or debugger depending on conflict type before implementation continues.
expected.delegation: Use debugger for ambiguous failure-source conflicts or reviewer for diff/review conflicts.
expected.escalation: Escalate only the conflict resolution slice to a stronger role/model when the evidence is contradictory.
expected.model_routing: Use gpt-5.5 debugger or reviewer for high-ambiguity conflict resolution.
expected.final_review: Root cites the conflict, the resolution evidence, and the accepted next action.
expected.runtime_fallback: worker for debugger fallback; explorer for reviewer fallback

expected_ledger.escalation: contradiction is routed to conflict resolution before continuing

prompt: /orchestrate An implementation agent broadened beyond its packet scope. Block completion and route correction.

expected.route: Final review blocks when a writer broadens beyond packet scope, then routes a correction or review packet.
expected.delegation: Use reviewer to confirm scope expansion and implementer/mechanic for bounded correction.
expected.escalation: Escalate to root decision or risk_controller if the expanded scope cannot be safely separated.
expected.model_routing: Use gpt-5.5 reviewer for high-impact scope review, then the cheapest safe correction agent.
expected.final_review: Root final review remains blocked until out-of-scope changes are reverted, justified, or explicitly accepted.
expected.runtime_fallback: explorer for review fallback; worker for correction fallback

expected_ledger.escalation: scope expansion blocks final review and routes bounded correction
expected_ledger.final_review: blocked until scope expansion is resolved
